sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **OpenClaw v2026.4.22 (Apr 23 release)** — Adds image gen / TTS / STT to the xAI provider (grok-imagine-image / grok-imagine-image-pro, reference-image editing, 6 live voices, MP3 / WAV / PCM / G.711, grok-stt, real-time voice-call transcription). `openclaw models auth login` in Models/auth changes to add to default models (resolving the bug where re-auth of OpenAI Codex blew away other providers' aliases / per-model params). Wraps Gateway/Linux child processes (supervisor / PTY / MCP stdio / browser) in `/bin/sh` shims for OOM-score isolation. Fixes Moonshot Kimi K2.6's OpenAI-compatible transport so it doesn't strictly sanitize native tool_call IDs (resolving the multi-turn agentic-flow breakdown after 2-3 rounds). Updates pi-bundled 0.68.1 to deliver the OpenCode Go catalog through pi (kimi-k2.6 / Qwen / GLM / MiMo / MiniMax updates). Bundles tokenjuice as opt-in (compresses exec / bash output during Pi-embedded execution).

citations.label: Patchbot - Patch Notes for OpenClaw

Runs on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 racks; API priced 2x GPT-5.4 but with "smarter, change back in token efficiency" framing. Specialized for multi-step planning / tool use / self-check.
**OpenAI GPT-5.5 official (Apr 23)** — Model lineage and positioning.

bullets.body: **Anthropic Claude Code quality post-mortem (Apr 23)** — Concludes that three changes compounded to drag down Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.6: 3/4 default flip from reasoning-effort high → medium (reverted 4/7), 3/26 idle-thinking-clear bug firing every turn (fixed), 4/16 verbosity-reduction system prompt (reverted 4/20). All fixed in v2.1.116, with usage caps reset across all subs on 4/23.

bullets.body: **DeepSeek V4 Preview: V4-Pro / V4-Flash released (Apr 24)** — V4-Pro: 1.6T total / 49B active. V4-Flash: 284B total / 13B active. Native **1M token context**. **Hybrid Attention Architecture** cuts inference FLOPs at 1M context to 27% of V3.2, KV cache to 10%. Both posted to Hugging Face as open weights, also accessible via DeepSeek API / chat.deepseek.com. Beats other open models on coding / math benchmarks; self-rates world knowledge as second only to Gemini 3.1-Pro, with a "3-6 months" gap to GPT-5.4 / Gemini 3.1-Pro.

**Simon Willison: DeepSeek V4 — almost frontier, fraction of price (Apr 24)** — Reviews the picture of GPT-5.4 / Gemini 3.1-Pro-grade performance at API prices an order of magnitude cheaper, including a pelican benchmark take.

sections.category: AI Security > Solutions: Zero-trust Access Control for Agentic AI

bullets.body: **Keycard: Control Plane specialized for AI agents (out of stealth Oct 2025 with $38M seed + Series A; product / M&A expansion through Q1-Q2 2026)** — Andreessen Horowitz / boldstart / Acrew Capital lead. The founding team comes from Snyk (Ian Livingstone / Matthew Creager) + Auth0 Chief Architect / Passport.js author (Jared Hanson) — an "identity pros" lineup. Builds **workload attestation (SPIFFE / k8s SA / cloud instance ID / mTLS) + composite identity for user / device / task**, and **replaces static secrets / API keys with dynamic / task-scoped / revocable tokens** so agents never see raw secrets. Tamper-resistant audit for every tool call / data access. CRN-named one of "10 Cloud Computing Startups to Watch in 2026." Acquired **Anchor.dev in March 2026** (extending coding-agent runtime security), acquired **Runebook** (SDK ecosystem expansion), and partnered with **Smallstep** to anchor runtime security to verified infrastructure.

citations.label: Keycard - The Control Plane for Autonomous Agents

bullets.body: **Keycard: governable coding agents via Anchor.dev acquisition (Apr 2026)** — Frames "coding agents are inherently ungovernable" and closes runtime identity / permission enforcement from build time to runtime.

citations.label: Keycard Blog - Coding Agents Are Ungovernable, We're Fixing That: Keycard acquires Anchor.dev

bullets.body: **Keycard × Smallstep: anchoring AI agent runtime security to verified infrastructure (Mar 23, 2026)** — Cryptographically binds workload identity to a verified-infrastructure layer; trust chains via distributed anchors rather than centralization.

citations.label: GlobeNewswire - Keycard and Smallstep Anchor AI Agent Runtime Security to Verified Infrastructure

bullets.body: **Keycard: CRN 2026 Cloud Computing Watch 10** — Recognized as a leading solution to the AI agent identity / access problem.

**Okta: Okta for AI Agents (GA 2026-04-30)** — Registers AI agents as "first-class identities" in a central directory, treating ownership / lifecycle / policy on par with human users. Continuous discovery of sanctioned / shadow AI agents, SIEM forwarding for tool-call / authorization / access logs, runtime enforcement via universal logout. GA from Apr 30.

citations.label: Okta Blog - Every Agent Needs an Identity: Introducing Okta for AI Agents

bullets.body: **Okta Showcase 2026: a new blueprint for the secure agentic enterprise** — Showcase 2026 unveils an enterprise refactoring direction centered on agent identity. Fortune's Apr 13 analysis frames it as "companies have started treating AI agents as employees, but the corporate structure still treats them as software."

citations.label: Okta Newsroom - Okta announces new blueprint for the secure agentic enterprise

bullets.body: **Cisco: Zero Trust Access extends to the agentic AI workforce** — Duo IAM extended, MCP policy enforcement, Secure Access with intent-aware monitoring. Identity / access / runtime behavior governed by a single architecture. UVP: countering hardcoded credentials / over-privilege / unpredictable behavior.

citations.label: Cisco Newsroom - Cisco Reimagines Security for the Agentic Workforce

citations.label: Cisco - Zero Trust for Your Agentic AI Workforce Solution Brief

citations.label: Microsoft Security Blog - Announcing Zero Trust for AI

bullets.body: **Microsoft: secure agentic AI end-to-end (continuing through April)** — End-to-end security strategy from agent dev to ops; clarifies the integration of Entra Agent ID / Purview / Defender for AI.

citations.label: Microsoft Security Blog - Secure agentic AI end-to-end

Provides agent-to-agent dialog / workflow-relationship maps / cross-system anomaly detection. Covers all 10 categories of OWASP Agentic Top 10. **Wins the 2026 Google Cloud Partner of the Year Award for Security: Analytics & Operations.**

bullets.body: **Intel Q1 2026 +20% beat (Apr 23)** — See Hardware section.
**Anthropic Q1 2026 annualized revenue $30B+ (Apr 22)** — Up 3x+ from $9B at year-end 2025. Above OpenAI's $25B. Multi-vendor compute contracts (TPU / Broadcom / Google Cloud) underwrite the surge.

**ServiceNow -18% post-print, worst-ever single-day drop (Apr 23)** — Apr 22's -12 to -14% widens to -18% on Apr 23, YTD 2026 -45%. The "AI displaces existing workflow SaaS" concern ripples to Salesforce / Workday / Oracle / IBM, becoming its own theme.
**OpenAI GPT-5.5 + Workspace Agents drive consumer-grade AI to $1B revenue daily** — Estimates from analyst notes suggest the OpenAI revenue base. ---
